body:
### Overview

This control identifies cases where an EKS node group role has ECR write permissions. Worker nodes usually need to pull container images, not push, delete, or mutate images in registries.

Restrict node roles to read-only ECR permissions and move image publishing permissions to CI/CD roles.
name: Minimize cluster access to read-only for Amazon ECR
remediationDescription:
## Remediation

### AWS

Remove Amazon ECR write permissions from EKS node group roles. This control identifies node group IAM roles that can push, delete, or upload image layers to ECR; nodes should normally only pull images.

1. Identify the IAM role used by the affected EKS node group.
2. Review attached and inline policies for ECR write actions such as ecr:PutImage, ecr:BatchDeleteImage, ecr:InitiateLayerUpload, ecr:UploadLayerPart, and ecr:CompleteLayerUpload.
3. Remove policies that grant ECR write access from the node role.
4. Attach AmazonEC2ContainerRegistryReadOnly or an equivalent least-privilege read-only policy.
5. Move image push permissions to CI/CD roles or release automation roles.
6. Re-run sync and assessment.

Review the node group role:

~~~bash
aws eks describe-nodegroup \
  --cluster-name <cluster-name> \
  --nodegroup-name <nodegroup-name> \
  --region {{asset.region}} \
  --query 'nodegroup.nodeRole'
~~~

Remove broad write policy and attach read-only pull access:

~~~bash
aws iam detach-role-policy \
  --role-name <node-role-name> \
  --policy-arn <ecr-write-policy-arn>

aws iam attach-role-policy \
  --role-name <node-role-name> \
  --policy-arn arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/AmazonEC2ContainerRegistryReadOnly
~~~

References:
- https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonECR/latest/userguide/security_iam_id-based-policy-examples.html
- https://docs.aws.amazon.com/eks/latest/userguide/create-node-role.html
- https://docs.aws.amazon.com/aws-managed-policy/latest/reference/AmazonEC2ContainerRegistryReadOnly.html

The control is satisfied when the EKS node group role no longer has ECR write actions.
description: Amazon EKS node group roles that can write to Amazon ECR instead of read-only pulling images.
